name: Guadiloba Reservoir Loop
name_original: Camino Sierra de Fuentes a Pantano Guadiloba – circular
description: This easy 12.5-mile mountain bike route from Sierra de Fuentes to Pantano Guadiloba offers diverse terrain and views of Cáceres.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cáceres offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features rolling hills, open plains, and areas around reservoirs, providing varied terrain for exploration. Riders can expect a mix of natural trails, rural paths, and some climbs, particularly towards elevated areas like Sierra De Fuentes. The landscape is characterized by its agricultural zones, scattered woodlands, and unique rock formations. Best mountain bike trails around Cáceres The most popular mountain bike trail is Subida al Risco de Sierra De Fuentes desde Cáceres – circular, a 18.6 miles (29.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views from the Sierra De Fuentes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los Barruecos and Casar de Cáceres Loop from Cáceres, a moderate 33.1 miles (53.2 km) path. It traverses the unique granite rock formations of Los Barruecos and rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Guadiloba Reservoir Loop, a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail leading through reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Cáceres is defined by rolling hills, reservoir paths, and open rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy mountain bike trails Cáceres to more advanced routes. The routes in Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 110 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cáceres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Cáceres?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Cáceres, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy routes to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cáceres?
A: Yes, Cáceres offers 13 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013844310/guadiloba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.5 miles (20.1 km) long and takes about 1 hour 33 minutes to complete, leading through pleasant reservoir surroundings.

Q: What are the options for advanced mountain bikers in Cáceres?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cáceres has 23 difficult mountain bike trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013496521/subida-al-risco-de-sierra-de-fuentes-desde-caceres-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subida al Risco de Sierra De Fuentes desde Cáceres – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, known for its challenging climbs and panoramic views from the Sierra De Fuentes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cáceres?
A: The terrain around Cáceres is diverse, featuring rolling hills, open plains, and areas around reservoirs. You'll encounter a mix of natural trails, rural paths, and some significant climbs, especially in elevated areas like Sierra De Fuentes. The landscape also includes agricultural zones, scattered woodlands, and unique rock formations, such as those found near Los Barruecos.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cáceres?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Cáceres are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013496521/subida-al-risco-de-sierra-de-fuentes-desde-caceres-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subida al Risco de Sierra De Fuentes desde Cáceres – circular&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008830942/los-barruecos-and-casar-de-caceres-loop-from-caceres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos and Casar de Cáceres Loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Cáceres?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and pass by interesting natural and historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008830942/los-barruecos-and-casar-de-caceres-loop-from-caceres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos and Casar de Cáceres Loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the unique granite rock formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6093807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might lead you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4343940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or offer glimpses of the region's agricultural landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Cáceres?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 110 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to reservoir paths, and the opportunity to explore open rural landscapes and unique rock formations.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: Yes, some routes offer proximity to historical areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31726460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeflores Mine – Historic Centre of Cáceres loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt; can bring you close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4755551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Centre of Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, where you can find landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3783241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Co-Cathedral of Santa María&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/780383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Main Square&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cáceres?
A: Cáceres experiences hot summers, so spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though some trails might be muddy after rain. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that include reservoir views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013844310/guadiloba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering scenic views as it takes you through the surroundings of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4343940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir – Historic Centre of Cáceres loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Cáceres typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013844310/guadiloba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes, while more challenging and longer trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008830942/los-barruecos-and-casar-de-caceres-loop-from-caceres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos and Casar de Cáceres Loop from Cáceres&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 4 hours.
tips: The reservoir was created by the reservoir of the Gualdiloba river in 1971. Next to the reservoir is the Guadipark recreational area. It is really big and has several facilities like swimming pools, restaurants, walking areas and an environmental area. The last time I went by it seemed closed.
Very fun section of the trail. Perfect for mountain biking but also doable with a gravel bike.

There is nothing special about this point where they cross the Gualdiloba, except that when there is water it is very beautiful, but if you follow the course of the river in the next bend you will find the ruins of an old stone bridge.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Route Sierra de Fuentes to Pantano Guadiloba – circular?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is rated as moderate. While it covers approximately 20.1 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 200 meters, it is generally considered suitable for all skill levels, offering a rewarding yet manageable experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied and dynamic terrain. You'll encounter natural paths, gravel sections, asphalt, and both paved and unpaved stretches. There's also a small amount of singletrack to keep things interesting.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect wonderful scenery, including panoramic views that allow for an identification photo of all the important buildings in Cáceres. A central feature is the Guadiloba Reservoir, known for its unique 'dragon' shape and ornithological interest. You'll also ride along the Trail Along the Gualdiloba and pass the Gualdiloba Stone Bridge.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see around the Guadiloba Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Guadiloba Reservoir is a significant area for birdwatching. You might spot species such as bustards, gray herons, common storks, bluebirds, and teals, among others.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: On average, the circular route takes about 1 hour and 33 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Route Sierra de Fuentes to Pantano Guadiloba – circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife sightings around the Guadiloba Reservoir. Always respect local regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Cáceres region of Extremadura can get quite warm in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, making them ideal times to enjoy the route's scenery and wildlife without extreme heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Route Sierra de Fuentes to Pantano Guadiloba – circular. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Sierra de Fuentes. You can typically find parking within the town of Sierra de Fuentes, near the beginning of the trail, making it convenient to start and finish your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes significant portions of the Camino de los Descubridores - Etapa 1 and the Camino de Cáceres a Sierra de Fuentes (por la umbría), as well as parts of the Cordel del Casar and Ronda Sur.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. A helmet, appropriate cycling attire, basic bike repair tools, and snacks are also advisable. Don't forget your camera for the panoramic views of Cáceres and the reservoir!
